title: epic_proposals Tabelle + CRUD-Endpoints
description: Neues Datenmodell für Epic-Proposals anlegen: Alembic-Migration für `epic_proposals`-Tabelle (id UUID PK, project_id FK, proposed_by FK, title, description, rationale, state [proposed|accepted|rejected], depends_on UUID[], resulting_epic_id FK nullable, rejection_reason, created_at, updated_at, version INT). Pydantic-Schemas: EpicProposalCreate, EpicProposalUpdate, EpicProposalResponse. REST-CRUD-Endpoints: `GET /api/epic-proposals?project_id=&state=` (paginated, triage/admin/developer), `POST /api/epic-proposals` (developer+), `PATCH /api/epic-proposals/{id}` (Proposer oder admin, Optimistic Locking), `POST /api/epic-proposals/{id}/accept` (triage/admin), `POST /api/epic-proposals/{id}/reject` (triage/admin, body: {reason}). Service-Layer in `app/services/epic_service.py` ergänzen. Audit-Log für alle mutierende Operationen. Index: `(project_id, state)` für schnelle Triage-Abfragen.

criteria: Alembic-Migration erstellt epic_proposals-Tabelle mit allen Spalten und Indexes
GET /api/epic-proposals filtert korrekt nach project_id und state
POST /api/epic-proposals/{id}/accept erstellt Epic und setzt resulting_epic_id
RBAC korrekt: propose ohne write_epics → 403; accept/reject ohne manage_proposals → 403
Unit-Test: CRUD, Accept→Epic erstellt, Reject→Notification, Optimistic Lock
